data.problem_definition: To provide a user-friendly and accessible web-based tool for performing basic and advanced mathematical calculations.

user_personas.name: Student Susan
user_personas.description: A high school or college student needing a quick and reliable tool for homework and understanding mathematical concepts.
goals: Solve math problems accurately and efficiently

user_personas.name: Professional Paul
user_personas.description: An engineer, scientist, or business professional requiring precise calculations for work-related tasks.
goals: Streamline workflow with quick access to results
Maintain a record of calculations for documentation

user_personas.name: Casual User Carol
user_personas.description: An everyday user who needs a simple calculator for common tasks like budgeting, shopping, or splitting bills.
needs: Basic arithmetic operations (addition, subtraction, multiplication, division)
goals: Avoid mistakes with clear input and output
Spend minimal time learning how to use the tool

core: Display of current input and result
advanced: Scientific operations (sin, cos, tan, log, exp, square root, power, factorial)
enhancement: Responsive design for various screen sizes (desktop, tablet, mobile)
Error handling for invalid inputs (e.g., division by zero, invalid operation)
Accessibility features (e.g., screen reader support, keyboard navigation)

mvp: Display of current input and result
Responsive design for various screen sizes (desktop, tablet, mobile)
phase_2: Scientific operations (sin, cos, tan, log, exp, square root, power, factorial)
Error handling for invalid inputs (e.g., division by zero, invalid operation)
phase_3: Accessibility features (e.g., screen reader support, keyboard navigation)

use_cases.title: Performing a Basic Arithmetic Calculation
use_cases.description: A user wants to add two numbers together.
preconditions: User has opened the web-based calculator application.
postconditions: The result '15' is displayed on the calculator screen.

use_cases.title: Using a Scientific Function
use_cases.description: A student needs to calculate the sine of an angle.
preconditions: User has opened the web-based calculator application.
postconditions: The sine of 30 (0.5) is displayed on the calculator screen.

use_cases.title: Clearing an Incorrect Entry
use_cases.description: A user has typed an incorrect number and wants to correct it without restarting the entire calculation.
preconditions: User has entered a number after an operation (e.g., '10 + 5' where '5' is incorrect).
postconditions: The last entered number ('5') is cleared, and the display shows '10 + ' ready for a new number input.

use_cases.title: Recalling a Previous Calculation from History
use_cases.description: A professional needs to review a calculation performed earlier in the session.
steps: User clicks on a previous calculation to recall it.
postconditions: The recalled calculation (numbers and operation) or its result is displayed on the main calculator screen, allowing for further operations.

user_flow: 1. **Access Application**: User navigates to the web-based calculator URL.
2. **Initial State**: Calculator displays '0' and is ready for input.
3. **Input Numbers/Operators**: User clicks on number buttons or types numbers using the keyboard. User clicks on operator buttons (+, -, *, /, etc.) or types them.
4. **Intermediate Display**: The display updates to show the current number and/or pending operation.
5. **Perform Calculation**: User clicks '=' button or another operator for chained operations.
6. **Display Result**: The result of the calculation is shown on the display.
7. **Clear/Correct**: User can use 'CE' to clear the last entry or 'C/AC' to clear all inputs and the current result.
8. **Continue/Exit**: User can continue with new calculations or close the browser tab/window.

in_scope: Standard arithmetic operations (addition, subtraction, multiplication, division).
Scientific functions (trigonometric, logarithmic, exponential, power, root, factorial).
Parentheses for controlling order of operations.
Keyboard support for numerical and operational input.
Responsive user interface for various device screen sizes.
User interface themes (e.g., light, dark mode).
Basic error handling (e.g., division by zero).
out_of_scope: Unit conversions (e.g., currency, temperature, length, weight).
Financial functions (e.g., loan calculators, present value, future value).
Integration with external data sources or APIs.
Offline functionality (PWA features for offline use).
User accounts or saving personal settings on a server.
Advanced data visualization beyond simple result display.
Time-based calculations (e.g., date differences, time zone conversions).
